title: boerhavia erecta var. thornberi
intro: Boerhavia erecta var. thornberi, also known as desert holly or horned poppy, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of gastrointestinal problems. In herbal preparations, Boerhavia erecta var. thornberi is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
